mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over a rolling 3-year period, the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is negative or below 0.05, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises from its low cycle level and crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that leads to reduced consumer spending and lower equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If in at least 1/3 of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05 against zero), this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) staying above 5.0% indicates tight monetary policy that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, prompting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as markets reprice risk quickly.
failure_condition: If over any 18-month window, the median 5-day S&P 500 return during periods with FEDFUNDS > 5.0% is not negative, or if the proportion of negative returns is less than 55%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals synchronized expansion in manufacturing and construction, which together predict robust economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint indicator (monthly INDPRO growth >0.5% AND HOUST growth >2%) does not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of occurrences, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Combined acceleration in real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand strength that drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer 60-day horizon as fundamentals improve.
failure_condition: If the combination of quarterly GDP growth >2% and monthly RSAFS growth >0.5% does not yield positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the Rank IC over a 5-year rolling window is below 0.02, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 4.0% and payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth is positive, the economy is in a late-cycle phase with rising wage pressures and limited slack, which historically precedes equity market corrections, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If during regimes with UNRATE < 4.0% and PAYEMS growth >0.2% month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative on average and does not underperform the unconditional mean by at least 1% annualized, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 300 and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 4.5%, the economy is in a high-inflation, tight-monetary regime that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: If in any contiguous 2-year period, the average 20-day S&P 500 return under this regime is positive or not significantly different from the unconditional mean (one-sample t-test p>0.1), this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp 1-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 (baseline 2017 level) signals a contraction in factory output that leads to downward revisions in corporate earnings and economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets adjust to incoming weakness.
failure_condition: If more than 40% of instances with INDPRO < 100 are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the average return is not below -0.5%, this hypothesis is rejected.
